# Portfolio Tracker Next.js Application

- [x] Verify that the copilot-instructions.md file in the .github directory is created.

- [x] Clarify Project Requirements
	Portfolio tracker application that integrates with Plaid API to fetch investment holdings

- [x] Scaffold the Project
	Next.js project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, ESLint, App Router, and src directory created

- [x] Customize the Project
	Converted Flask Python application functionality to Next.js with API routes and React components

- [x] Install Required Extensions
	No specific extensions required for Next.js

- [x] Compile the Project
	Dependencies installed and project builds without errors

- [x] Create and Run Task
	Development server running successfully on http://localhost:3000

- [x] Launch the Project
	Next.js development server is running with Plaid integration

- [x] Ensure Documentation is Complete
	README.md updated with complete project information

## Setup Complete

The Portfolio Tracker Next.js application has been successfully converted from the original Flask Python application. 

### Key Features Implemented:
- Plaid API integration for brokerage account connections
- React components with Plaid Link for account authentication  
- API routes for token creation and holdings retrieval
- Formatted console output for portfolio data
- Modern responsive UI with Tailwind CSS

### To Use:
1. Add your Plaid credentials to `.env.local`
2. The development server is running on http://localhost:3000
3. Click "Connect Your Brokerage Account" to start

All functionality from the original test.py Flask application has been successfully ported to Next.js.
